description_long: This is a tall, post-mounted lantern with a square lantern head perched atop a slender, vertical metal pole. The lantern head features a slightly flared, boxy design with a weathered, dark metallic finish and a central translucent panel, likely for light emission. The pole is straight and narrow, connecting the lantern head to a flat, square base that provides stability. The surface of the lantern appears aged, with visible patina and discoloration, suggesting it is made of metal and possibly glass. The lantern is utilitarian in design, with minimal decorative elements, making it suitable for urban streets, parks, or sidewalks. Its construction and proportions indicate it is intended for outdoor use, providing illumination in public spaces.
description: A tall, weathered metal lantern with a square head and translucent panel, mounted on a slender pole with a square base. Its utilitarian design suits urban outdoor environments.
description_view_0: Front view showing the square lantern head with a central translucent panel and the full length of the slender pole and square base.
description_view_1: Side view revealing the boxy profile of the lantern head and the straight alignment of the pole and base.
description_view_2: Rear view similar to the front, but the translucent panel is less prominent, emphasizing the lantern's symmetrical design.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, mirroring view 1, with the same boxy lantern head and slender pole visible.
